paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces segment-level recurrence mechanism to enable modeling dependencies beyond fixed-length contexts while preserving temporal coherence, combined with relative positional encoding to avoid ambiguity in absolute positions.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Unspecified large-scale text corpora (mentioned as 'a large-scale text corpus')
raw_extraction.application: Long-text generation, character-level and word-level language modeling, capturing long-range context
raw_extraction.research_problem: Modeling long-term dependencies in sequential data without fixed-length context fragmentation
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with segment-level recurrence and relative positional encoding
raw_extraction.extension: Segment-level recurrence mechanism enables reuse of hidden states from previous segments, resolving context fragmentation and allowing modeling of dependencies beyond fixed-length limits.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.innovation: **Segment-level recurrence mechanism** enables modeling long-term dependencies across segments, combined with **relative positional encodings** to eliminate fixed-length context limitations. These innovations allow efficient handling of variable-length contexts without quadratic complexity.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: WikiText-103, enwik8, text8, One Billion Word, Penn Treebank
raw_extraction.application: Long-document processing, text generation, language modeling, sequence modeling
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (not explicitly stated but inferred from publication context)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Modeling long-term dependencies in sequential data beyond fixed-length context windows
raw_extraction.extension: Segment-level recurrence mechanism and relative positional encodings enable long-term dependency modeling without quadratic computational overhead.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.innovation: **Segment-level recurrence mechanism** and **relative positional encodings** enable long-term dependency modeling with linear scalability, achieving state-of-the-art perplexity on benchmark datasets.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: WikiText-103, One Billion Word, Penn Treebank
raw_extraction.application: Text generation, language modeling, long-document processing
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (not explicitly stated but inferred from publication context)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalable long-term dependency modeling in language models
raw_extraction.extension: Relative positional encodings reduce memory usage and enable longer effective context lengths during evaluation.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.innovation: **Segment-level recurrence mechanism** and **relative positional encodings** enable significant improvements in perplexity over baselines, demonstrating superior long-term dependency modeling capabilities.
raw_extraction.application: Text generation, language modeling, long-document processing
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (not explicitly stated but inferred from publication context)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Achieving state-of-the-art performance in long-term dependency modeling with fewer parameters
raw_extraction.extension: Recurrence mechanism resolves context fragmentation issues, improving generalization across long sequences.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.innovation: **Deep 24-layer Transformer-XL architecture** with **segment-level recurrence** and **relative positional encodings**, achieving new state-of-the-art perplexity scores on WikiText-103 and One Billion Word datasets.
raw_extraction.application: Text generation, language modeling, long-document processing
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (not explicitly stated but inferred from publication context)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scaling transformer architectures to deep configurations while maintaining long-term dependency modeling capabilities
raw_extraction.extension: Combines segment-level recurrence with relative positional encodings to achieve superior performance on long sequences.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.innovation: **Lightweight 12-layer Transformer-XL** with **segment-level recurrence** and **relative positional encodings**, designed for efficient long-term dependency modeling with minimal parameter overhead.
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (not explicitly stated but inferred from publication context)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient long-term dependency modeling with limited computational resources
raw_extraction.extension: Uses adaptive softmax and input representations to further optimize efficiency.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.organization: University of Toronto / DeepMind / NYU / Carnegie Mellon University
raw_extraction.innovation: **Segment-level recurrence** enables handling arbitrarily long sequences by maintaining a fixed-size memory buffer of past segments, combined with **relative positional embeddings** to efficiently compute attention across extended contexts without quadratic complexity.
raw_extraction.application: Long-range dependency modeling, text generation, language understanding with extended context
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (no explicit license mentioned in paper)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Extending the effective context window of Transformer models beyond fixed-length limits while maintaining computational efficiency.
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with segment-level recurrence
raw_extraction.extension: **Relative positional embeddings** reduce attention computation cost from quadratic to linear time by leveraging discrete distance buckets between tokens.
